Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.

Node started: Problem Formalization: Geometric Rectangle Packing Optimization 1. Mathematical Definition Given: A bounding box B = [0, W] x [0, H], where W=10000, H=10000. A set of N advertisers A = {a1, a2, ..., an}. For each ai, a target point Pi = (xi, yi) and a target area Si. Variables: For each ai, a rectangle Ri = [xi,min, xi,max] x [yi,min, yi,max]. Constraints: Containment: For all i: Pi is in Ri. Boundary: For all i: Ri is a subset of B. Non-overlapping (Hard): For all i not equal to j: Ri intersection Rj is empty. Objective Function (Maximize Satisfaction): Maximize Z = sum of f(Ri, Si) from i=1 to N, where f is a penalty function measuring the deviation of Area(Ri) from Si. A common form is f(Ri, Si) = exp(-|Area(Ri) - Si| / sigma) or a linear penalty 1 - (|Area(Ri) - Si| / Si). 2. Algorithmic Strategy Since this is a packing problem with target areas, a pure greedy approach will likely lead to fragmentation. We propose a Hierarchical Partitioning + Local Refinement strategy. Stage 1: Initial Construction (Recursive Quadrant Partitioning) 1. Sort advertisers by a priority metric (e.g., Si descending or proximity to center). 2. Use a KD-Tree-like recursive decomposition. For a given region R, pick an advertiser ai whose Pi is near the center of R. 3. Assign ai a rectangle Ri centered at Pi with area Si (or as large as possible within R without hitting boundaries). 4. Split the remaining area of R into smaller rectangular sub-regions and recurse. Stage 2: Iterative Improvement (Local Search / Simulated Annealing) 1. Expansion/Contraction: For a selected ai, attempt to grow Ri to better match Si by shifting its boundaries until it hits another rectangle Rj or the boundary B. 2. Swap/Re-partition: If two rectangles Ri, Rj are highly unsatisfied, attempt
